subject: Provisional Tax
body_text: Afternoon everyone, In all the running around with Bern leaving and finalising the 2020 year end, we never attended to the provisional tax for the First Period 2021. The attached IRP6's are for Regima Skin Treatments and Regima World Wide Distribution. All other entities (as well as the personal returns for DJ Faucitt, PA Faucitt and J Faucitt) have Zero provisional tax and Nil returns have already been submitted. I've reduced the Regima Skin Treatments assessment drastically based on the final results for 2020.  Pete, if you can give me the go ahead to submit these returns I will load these for payment as well. I also attach the Statement of Account for Pete's personal taxes. The R15,343.52 is payable but unfortunately I cannot load this payment via e-filing. The payment must please be loaded on your side with the exact payment reference number at the bottom of the document. Please let me know if anything is unclear. Kind regards, Danie Bantjes

subject: Re: Provisional Tax
body_text: Hi everyone Here's the Payment confirmations for SARS provisional tax, ready for your release on FNB anytime up to close of business on Monday. I've tweaked the numbers since our discussion yesterday: On RWWD I've made use of the Covid Relief payment deferment, this way we only have to pay R49k and not R165k. On RST I also utilised the Covid relief but in such a way that we could submit the correctly based estimate taxable income and still end up paying R219k. So no questions will be asked. The Covid relief deferment on provisional tax works like this: For the 1st period provisional tax we can reduce the tax payable to 15% of the total tax payable For the 2nd period provisional tax we can reduce the tax payable by 35% less what we paid in the 1st period We then have up to September 2021 to do catch-up. I strongly recommend we make use of this relief as it not only saves a bunch of cash but also is interest and penalty free. So my view is let the money rather stay in our bank and earn interest than to let it sit with SARS and earn nothing. Kind regards, Danie Bantjes On Wed, 26 Aug 2020 at 13:30, Danie Bantjes < danie.bantjes@gmail.com > wrote: Afternoon everyone, In all the running around with Bern leaving and finalising the 2020 year end, we never attended to the provisional tax for the First Period 2021. The attached IRP6's are for Regima Skin Treatments and Regima World Wide Distribution. All other entities (as well as the personal returns for DJ Faucitt, PA Faucitt and J Faucitt) have Zero provisional tax and Nil returns have already been submitted. I've reduced the Regima Skin Treatments assessment drastically based on the final results for 2020.  Pete, if you can give me the go ahead to submit these returns I will load these for payment as well. I also attach the Statement of Account for Pete's personal taxes. The R15,343.52 is payable but unfortunately I cannot load this payment via e-filing. The payment must please be loaded on your side

subject: FW: Important Notice SARS
body_text: Hi Danie,   Ek dink jy doen die provisional taxes vir Jax, Peter and Danny.  Is ek reg?   Hier is ‘n notice van SARS indien jy dit nog nie gesien het nie.   Groete       From: BGR Alluvium < news@bgralluvium.co.za > Sent: Friday, 29 January 2021 12:07 PM To: jax@regima.com Subject: Important Notice   VIEW ONLINE > SHARE: Important Notice 29 January 2021   SARS EXTENDS FILING SEASON PROVISIONAL TAXPAYER DEADLINE FOR ONLINE FILERS Pretoria, Thursday 28 January 2021 – the South African Revenue Service (SARS) has noted with deep concern the unprecedented health challenge manifested through COVID-19 with the accompanying loss of lives and livelihoods. SARS wishes to convey its sincere and deepest condolences to those that have lost their loved ones and with a speedy recovery to those who are convalescing at home or in hospital. In light of these challenges, SARS has taken a decision to extend the Filling Season deadline for provisional taxpayers. Which is currently set for 29 January 2021, to  15 February 2021 . In line with SARS’ communication on steps taken to prevent the spread of COVID19 and protect both the taxpayer and SARS staff, taxpayers are encouraged to use eFiling or the SARS MobiApp. SARS Commissioner Edward Kieswetter said, “The decision to extend the Filing Season, was taken with due consideration of the traumatic effects being experience by taxpayers who have lost loved ones, as well as businesses who have lost staff, during the devastating second wave of Covid-19. The Commissioner also urges taxpayers who received auto- assessment notices last year but who have not yet accepted the auto-assessments, or edited and filed a return in response to the notice, to make use of this opportunity to do so. The Commissioner urged all these taxpayers to comply with their legal obligations by 15 February 2021, as it is vital that all taxpayers remain complaint. Failure to comply with their legal obligatio

subject: Provisional tax - RegimA Skin Treatment
body_text: Hi Pete I tried to phone you. I'm afraid I was way too optimistic yesterday when I said there was no provisional tax to pay. I completely forgot that we received a Covid-19 relief extension in August last year when we did the first provisional payment for RegimA Skin Treatment. SARS allowed us to "postpone" R511,201.52 of the amount we were supposed to pay. We now need to catch this up. So, in submitting a reduced estimate of taxable income based on our discussion yesterday as a result of reduced taxable income for 2021 tax year, the net result is that we now have to pay provisional tax for this period of R326,913.64 . Apologies for the confusion (I'll blame myself and Covid). At least we can expect some handsome refunds from SARS shortly as well so we should not be out of pocket. Please load the payment by tomorrow latest using the payment reference number 9052553840P00022021 Phone me when you have a chance please. Kind regards, Danie Bantjes

body_text: Hi Rynette That's good to hear. About time. I've checked on the statuses of RST and RWWD.  The accounts both still reflect the credit balances.  There is no correspondence indicating what the reason is for the refund not to be done or whether they want to perform an audit before refunding or requesting further information. On RWWD there was a request to update bank details. I've checked and the bank account details are still the same. Maybe please just confirm the account number again so I can double check. Make it for both RWWD and RST please. I'll keep checking regularly. I suspect the notorious "SARS 21 working days" rule may be at play here. subject: Re: FW: Regima Skin- IT14sd- 2020.
body_text: Hi Rynette/Anton 1. PG1 of IT14 specifically says turnover and ALL OTHER INCOME must be stated here: which is what was done. The R23,784,171 includes Other non-Vatable Interest received of R1,962,112 (declared as such separately on the IT14) which leaves R21,822,059 as the Turnover for the year which in turn is per the books of the company. 2. The VAT turnover was reconciled by Bernadine and I checked the block 1's on all the VAT201's and it all agreed. See attached page 2 of all VAT201's for the year as well as the reconciliation done by bernadien and checked by me. Not sure where you got your R23,640,730 from? 3. Therefore, the financial statements agree to the books as well as line 1 & 2 of all the VAT201's. 3. So, this brings us back to our initial discussion: the only issue we see is with the R615,000 Output tax that was declared on line 12 on the 202002 VAT201. How do we deal with this as we have no idea where this came from and it doesn't agree to any turnover or invoicing for the year? If this was a correction of any "under declaration" then surely this could just have been done on line one in the last period. What is concerning is that Bernadine never highlighted this to me or reflected this in her reconciliations of turnover presented to me. We were hoping you may have a logical explanation for this or recall a discussion with Bernadine about it as it throws the revenue for the company out by R4,715,000 for the year for which there is no evidence to substantiate this whatsoever. Any ideas on how to resolve this the correct way? Kind regards, Danie Bantjes On Wed, 21 Apr 2021 at 13:28, Rynette Farrar <rynette@regima.zone> wrote: Hi Danie   See email from Anton below.   Thanks     From: Anton < ahechter@telkomsa.net > Sent: Wednesday, 21 April 2021 13:27 To: Rynette Farrar <rynette@regima.zone> Cc: JAX@REGIMA.COM ; PETE@REGIMA.COM ; Jacqui Faucitt <jax@regima.zone> Subject: RE: Regima Skin-

subject: Regima IT14SD -2020
body_text: Hi Anton I had a long hard look at your proposed changes to the financial statements as well as the ITR14 and IT14SD. In turn, as the R615,000 variance in Output Vat seems to be the issue, I would recommend that we change the Sales as well as COS per the income statement by adding R4,100,000 to both lines, being the Ex Vat sales on R615,000 of Output VAT. I've attached an amended set of financial statements in line with this. No changes to the profits or anything else required this way.  I have then updated the ITR14 as well as IT14SD on e-filing to agree with all these changes and saved the returns. Please have a look to see if this now makes sense and will pass the IT14SD test. If you're happy, you can either submit the returns yourself or give me the go-ahead to do so. Kind regards, Danie Bantjes

subject: RE: RegimA World Wide
body_text: Good morning Danie,   Thanks for the email.  I will attend to it as soon as I am back at the office.   Myself tested positive last Sunday for the Delta strain.  I was as sick as a dog, for nearly a week, barely moving, as every time I moved, I wanted to throw up.  Just sleeping in a bundle for days on end.  I feel a lot better now.  Still getting tired, and having to have oxygen now and then, but I feel a bit more human again.  My son and husband also got it from me.  The isolation period for the Delta strain is 14 days.  I just can’t wait to get out of this house again, and back to normality.   Hope you and your wife recover soon.   Thanks       From: Danie Bantjes <danie.bantjes@gmail.com> Sent: Wednesday, 07 July 2021 10:04 To: Rynette Farrar <rynette@regima.zone>; Peter Andrew Faucitt <pete@regima.com>; Jacqui Faucitt <jax@regima.zone> Subject: RegimA World Wide   Morning Rynette   Attached some correspondence for RWWD for your attention.   Just so you know, I will not be able to come past this week to pick up the Villa Via files. subject: Vat Regima Skin Treatments Period ending September 2020 - double payments??
body_text: Good morning Anton,   It seems to me that the Vat for Regima Skin Treatments for the period ending September 2020 was paid twice.   I do not have access to the Vat portal, and therefore cannot investigate this myself.   One payment was made from Regima Skin Treatments, and the other payment from Regima World Wide.   Both amounts are for R169 825.52, and both amounts were paid on the 28 th of October 2020.   I have two different payment notifications that were created – 0061991207 and 0061991330   Can you please urgently look into this, and revert back to Danie and myself?   Regards

subject: Vat Regima Skin Treatments Period ending September 2020 - double payments??
body_text: Good morning Anton,   It seems to me that the Vat for Regima Skin Treatments for the period ending September 2020 was paid twice.   I do not have access to the Vat portal, and therefore cannot investigate this myself.   One payment was made from Regima Skin Treatments, and the other payment from Regima World Wide.   Both amounts are for R169 825.52, and both amounts were paid on the 28 th of October 2020.   I have two different payment notifications that were created – 0061991207 and 0061991330   Can you please urgently look into this, and revert back to Danie and myself?   Regards

subject: RE: Vat Regima Skin Treatments Period ending September 2020 - double payments??
body_text: Good morning Anton,   First of all, I never asked you about the double payment of the Vat for the period 09/2020, and you never told me about this mishap, or that it was repaid. Please if I am wrong here forward that email to me as I seriously don’t know where you get it that I queried this same issue three times now.  What I did email you about for this period was the verification documents for the verification audit that SARS was conducting.  I will forward that email correspondence to you again, so that you can enlighten yourself.  This correspondence does not contain anything to do with the double payment of the Vat for that same period.   Secondly I do not appreciate your sarcastic tone when I ask you something.   I would not ask you anything, if I had access to the Vat portal.   I treat you with respect, and I expect at least the same treatment back.   Regards       From: Anton <ahechter@telkomsa.net> Sent: Tuesday, 17 August 2021 09:08 To: Rynette Farrar <rynette@regima.zone>; 'Danie Bantjes' <danie.bantjes@gmail.com> Cc: JAX@REGIMA.COM; PETE@REGIMA.COM; Jacqui Faucitt <jax@regima.zone> Subject: RE: Vat Regima Skin Treatments Period ending September 2020 - double payments??   Good, Moring   It’s now the 3 time that you query this, it’s been resolve see attached proof the R 169,825.52 has been paid back, on the 07/03/21 , and correct figures on correct Company was mistake that’s been corrected. Please accept that the query has been resolve.   Cheers     From: Rynette Farrar [ mailto:rynette@regima.zone ] Sent: Tuesday, August 17, 2021 8:01 AM To: Anton; Danie Bantjes Cc: JAX@REGIMA.COM ; PETE@REGIMA.COM ; Jacqui Faucitt Subject: Vat Regima Skin Treatments Period ending September 2020 - double payments??   Good morning Anton,   It seems to me that the Vat for Regima Skin Treatments for the period ending September 2020 was paid twice. &nbsp

body_text: Good morning Anton,   First of all, I never asked you about the double payment of the Vat for the period 09/2020, and you never told me about this mishap, or that it was repaid. Please if I am wrong here forward that email to me as I seriously don’t know where you get it that I queried this same issue three times now.  What I did email you about for this period was the verification documents for the verification audit that SARS was conducting.  I will forward that email correspondence to you again, so that you can enlighten yourself.  This correspondence does not contain anything to do with the double payment of the Vat for that same period.   Secondly I do not appreciate your sarcastic tone when I ask you something.   I would not ask you anything, if I had access to the Vat portal.   I treat you with respect, and I expect at least the same treatment back.   Regards       From: Anton <ahechter@telkomsa.net> Sent: Tuesday, 17 August 2021 09:08 To: Rynette Farrar <rynette@regima.zone>; 'Danie Bantjes' <danie.bantjes@gmail.com> Cc: JAX@REGIMA.COM; PETE@REGIMA.COM; Jacqui Faucitt <jax@regima.zone> Subject: RE: Vat Regima Skin Treatments Period ending September 2020 - double payments??   Good, Moring   It’s now the 3 time that you query this, it’s been resolve see attached proof the R 169,825.52 has been paid back, on the 07/03/21 , and correct figures on correct Company was mistake that’s been corrected. Please accept that the query has been resolve.   Cheers     From: Rynette Farrar [ mailto:rynette@regima.zone ] Sent: Tuesday, August 17, 2021 8:01 AM To: Anton; Danie Bantjes Cc: JAX@REGIMA.COM ; PETE@REGIMA.COM ; Jacqui Faucitt Subject: Vat Regima Skin Treatments Period ending September 2020 - double payments??   Good morning Anton,   It seems to me that the Vat for Regima Skin Treatments for the period ending September 2020 was paid twice. &nbsp

subject: Intercompany loan accounts
body_text: Good afternoon Danie,   Herewith the details for the intercompany loan accounts:     Between Villa Via and Strategic :   These two loan accounts balance, except for a two hundred rand difference in the balance brought forward which would obviously a problem coming forward from the previous year.     Between Villa Via and Regima Skin Treatments:   There is a balance brought forward from the previous year of R109 896.92 which sit as a loan account in Villa’s books, but there is no balance brought forward for Villa Via in Regima’s books.     Between Regima World Wide and Regima Skin Treatments:   In Regima World Wide’s books, there is an amount of R169 825.52 as a loan to Regima Skin Treatments for the payment of their VAT for the period 09/2020.    This was paid in error and it was refunded on the 08/03/2021.  To cancel this out - Debit account 9300/000 and Credit account 5464/000.  Then the loan account will balance.     Between Regima Skin Treatment and Strategic:   The opening balance brough forward between the two accounts is out with R0.11   It seems that Bern used to sometimes posts the intercompany loans to the loan accounts, and then sometimes she use to post it to either the supplier and/or the customer account.  I will thus tell you what she has done.   On the 1/08/20 she posted a loan from Strategic to Regima Skin Treatments as a loan amount in Regima’s books, but in Strategic’s books she posted it to the supplier account, instead of to the loan account.  To rectify this in Strategic’s books, we need to Debit account 5466/000 and credit the supplier account for Regima Skin Treatments with R 300 000,00 date 01/08/20.   The R500 000 on the 01/08/21 was a transfer between Regima’s cheque account and their money market account, and it was not a loan to Strategic.  To fix this we will have to Debit account 5466/000 and Credit account 9990/000 with R

Kind regards, Danie Bantjes On Wed, 8 Sept 2021 at 15:52, Anton < ahechter@telkomsa.net > wrote: See awaiting info not received   From: Anton [mailto: ahechter@telkomsa.net ] Sent: Wednesday, August 25, 2021 10:21 AM To: 'Danie Bantjes'; 'Rynette Farrar'; 'Peter Andrew Faucitt'; 'Jacqui Faucitt' Subject: RE: Regima IT14SD -2020   Hi, About the Credit query, please adjust your Income statement to declare the extra Income on that issue on the Vat.   The IT14sd can’t filed   From: Anton [mailto: ahechter@telkomsa.net ] Sent: T hursday, May 06, 2021 10:27 AM To: 'Danie Bantjes'; 'Rynette Farrar'; 'Peter Andrew Faucitt'; 'Jacqui Faucitt' Subject: RE: Regima IT14SD -2020   Hi, Hope you are well, please need to change the Income Statement to add the R 410,000.00, so I can upload already revise.   Cheers         From: Danie Bantjes [mailto: danie.bantjes@gmail.com ] Sent: Tuesday, May 04, 2021 3:17 PM To: ahechter@telkomsa.net ; Rynette Farrar; Peter Andrew Faucitt; Jacqui Faucitt Subject: Regima IT14SD -2020   Hi Anton   I had a long hard look at your proposed changes to the financial statements as well as the ITR14 and IT14SD.   In turn, as the R615,000 variance in Output Vat seems to be the issue, I would recommend that we change the Sales as well as COS per the income statement by adding R4,100,000 to both lines, being the Ex Vat sales on R615,000 of Output VAT.   I've attached an amended set of financial statements in line with this. No changes to the profits or anything else required this way.    I have then updated the ITR14 as well as IT14SD on e-filing to agree with all these changes and saved the returns.   Please have a look to see if this now makes sense an

subject: FW: Vat - Regima WWD & Villa Via Dec 2021 & Jan 2022 & Regima Skin Treatments Jan 2022
body_text: Good morning Danie   Anton made a mistake on the Vat for Regima Skin Treatments.  See what he would like to do below, and let me know if you are okay with it or not?   Thanks     From: Anton <ahechter@telkomsa.net> Sent: Thursday, 24 February 2022 19:00 To: Rynette Farrar <rynette@regima.zone>; 'antonhechter793' <antonhechter793@gmail.com> Cc: JAX@REGIMA.COM; PETE@REGIMA.COM; Jacqui Faucitt <jax@regima.zone> Subject: RE: Vat - Regima WWD & Villa Via Dec 2021 & Jan 2022 & Regima Skin Treatments Jan 2022   Good, day   See attached pg1 towards the Vat201.    Regima Skin Treatments sow made mistake Zero rated declare wrongly instead declare the Expense ( no excuse )- human error must start wearing classes. On the Output if you see your summary stated Vat Included   Amount of R 2,299,016.00 X15/115 =  Vat  R 299,871.67 (not  R 245,525.03) so I use the Vat and work backwards to establish the Sales included. Will take the punch for the incorrect Zero rated, but the Sales according to the vat is fine because the payment is correct. If I going to do the correction will be out for Verification Audit, although won’t be problem.. Please may I ask for the overstated  Zero rated sales to take it off from the next submission with explanation  note, so the IT14sd will balance.   My apologies won’t happen again.   Cheers      From: Rynette Farrar [ mailto:rynette@regima.zone ] Sent: Thursday, February 24, 2022 7:44 AM To: ahechter@telkomsa.net ; 'antonhechter793' Cc: JAX@REGIMA.COM ; PETE@REGIMA.COM ; Jacqui Faucitt Subject: RE: Vat - Regima WWD & Villa Via Dec 2021 & Jan 2022 & Regima Skin Treatments Jan 2022   Good morning Anton   Can you please have another look at Regima Skin Treatment’s Output Vat?  I am not happy with it.  The Standard rate must be R1 890 025.26 and not R1 882 358.00, and look at your total

subject: VAT Strategic
body_text: Hi Danie   Probleem nommer een – jy sal sien die TB en die Vat report stem nie oor een nie.  Die verskil is in die invoices wat ek gemerk het op die Vat report by die Standard Output Vat.  Hierdie invoices, vir een of ander rede, sit nie onder die sales op die TB nie.  Wat sal ek hier doen?   Probleem nommer twee – ons het hier ‘n refund en SARS gaan vra vir supporting documents.  Nou die exports sal jy sien is nul, want die invoices was gekanselleer.  Ek het UK eers geinvoice in die volgende maand, en ook net vir die helfte van die bedrag.  Die R14 172.17 is vir die vlug koste en die R165 181.69 vir die produkte.  Sal ek die exports op hierdie form verklaar soos dit moet wees of nie?  Hou in gedagte dat as SARS vra vir supporting dokumente hulle gaan sien die exports is gekanselleer.  Hulle mag dalk begin vrae vra.   Dan moet ons vining voor die einde van die maand sit met hierdie exports, want ek sit met drie exports wat geinvoice moet word.   Groete

subject: Re: VAT Strategic
body_text: Hi Rynette I've had a look: 1. The invoices and credit notes that are on the report but not in the GL on account 1000/000 are: SIN30589;SIN30592; SIN30598; SCN30063.       The only logical explanation is that these invoices must have been allocated to a different GL account than Acc 1000/000. Please check the sales journal? 2. Agree with you, a refund will certainly trigger a VAT verification exercise, which we don't want, especially not for R26k considering all the little issues that may trip us up. So my suggestion is the following:     a. Declare all the exports as it should be as well as the revenue as per the report.     b. Reduce the Input VAT for this period and make a note to claim it in the following period:          I suggest removing the following 2 invoices: Doc no SS131827 - PRIME - VAT R13,271.35 as well as Doc No SS131829 - PRIME - VAT R20,070.00. 3. This will result in having to pay R6,988.04 to SARS, but by doing so hopefully avoiding a messy verification process which we will have difficulty in passing unscathed. I hope this helps? Kind regards, Danie Bantjes On Tue, 24 May 2022 at 09:55, Rynette Farrar <rynette@regima.zone> wrote: Hi Danie   Probleem nommer een – jy sal sien die TB en die Vat report stem nie oor een nie.  Die verskil is in die invoices wat ek gemerk het op die Vat report by die Standard Output Vat.  Hierdie invoices, vir een of ander rede, sit nie onder die sales op die TB nie.  Wat sal ek hier doen?   Probleem nommer twee – ons het hier ‘n refund en SARS gaan vra vir supporting documents.  Nou die exports sal jy sien is nul, want die invoices was gekanselleer.  Ek het UK eers geinvoice in die volgende maand, en ook net vir die helfte van die bedrag.  Die R14 172.17 is vir die vlug koste en die R165 181.69 vir die produkte.  Sal ek die exports op hierdie form verklaar soos dit moet wees of nie?  Hou in gedagte d

subject: Hug??
body_text: Good morning Danie   Don’t know what you think about this answer, but I think Anton think none of us have ever filed Vat returns and declare imports/exports.  Wonder how he filed the exports Vat for Regima before when he claims the custom code was not on the system.   Lot of nonsense – I guess he has to come up with some sort of excuse.   Regards     From: Anton <ahechter@telkomsa.net> Sent: Tuesday, 31 May 2022 17:47 To: Rynette Farrar <rynette@regima.zone>; 'antonhechter793' <antonhechter793@gmail.com> Cc: 'Danie Bantjes' <danie.bantjes@gmail.com>; Daniel Faucitt <d@rzo.io>; JAX@REGIMA.COM; PETE@REGIMA.COM; Jacqui Faucitt <jax@regima.zone> Subject: RE: Vat Regima April 2022 - You did not file the export total like I asked you to do   Good, afternoon   See amended Vat201 for the declaration of the Zero rated Export sales, what happen the Customs code wasn’t on the system so it auto remove the amount- code now declare, amount taken into account 20583237 Go back and see the first copy I send now Customs code, next time I will double check if the code is on the system, see Strategic code is there   I am glad you double check   Cheers   From: Rynette Farrar [ mailto:rynette@regima.zone ] Sent: Tuesday, May 31, 2022 8:13 AM To: ahechter@telkomsa.net ; 'antonhechter793' Cc: Danie Bantjes; Daniel Faucitt; JAX@REGIMA.COM ; PETE@REGIMA.COM ; Jacqui Faucitt Subject: RE: Vat Regima April 2022 - You did not file the export total like I asked you to do   Good morning Anton,   You have not stipulated the total for the exports on the Regima Vat return like I specifically ask you in my email below to make sure you record it correctly.   SARS are already sending enquiries to Danie re the exports totals that don’t add up.   You know what, I do not appreciate you continuously stuffing up, and that in my name on the VAT reports.    Regards     From: Anton

subject: Re: Anton to rectify all his mistakes
body_text: Hi Rynette Let's first do the VAT revenue reconciliations, comparing the VAT201 declarations to the GL for the Feb22 financial yea r. Then, go back from March22 and verify that all VAT submissions were done correctly. Being in the new financial year, these could possibly be rectified without too many repercussions, otherwise we could just rectify any year-to-date discrepancies in one of the future months. Going forward, now we are aware of the issue, you must please verify all the VAT201 submissions on a monthly basis. Being in the new financial year, these could possibly be rectified without too many repercussions, otherwise we could just rectify any year-to-date discrepancies in one of the future months.   Going forward, now we are aware of the issue, you must please verify all the VAT201 submissions on a monthly basis.   Kind regards,   Danie Bantjes     On Wed, 1 Jun 2022 at 07:36, Rynette Farrar < rynette@regima.zone > wrote: Hi Danie   Yes, I have it the same a you.  The warning flashes when the code is missing.  However, if you do not put in an amount, I guess no warning will flash.   Do you want Anton to go and rectify all where he has done wrong in the last 12 months?  Don’t you think this may raise red flags if he does that?   Regards     From: Danie Bantjes < danie.bantjes@gmail.com > Sent: Wednesday, 01 June 2022 07:29 To: Rynette Farrar < rynette@regima.zone > Cc: JAX@REGIMA.COM ; PETE@REGIMA.COM ; Jacqui Faucitt < jax@regima.zone > Subject: Re: Hug??   Hi Rynette   Anton may be correct, however last time I did it, when you push the file button and the customs code was missing it flashes a warning to insert the code.   Nonetheless, Anto

subject: FW: Exports - SARS
body_text: Hi Danie   Just want to explain that all the exports to EU goes through Strategic Logistics, as this company is the only one that are registered for the EURO1 certificate.   Regima Skin have all the sellable stock, and Strategic only have the packaging as stock.   Exports leave SA under Strategic, but get invoiced by Regima Skin.   Regards     From: Rynette Farrar Sent: Tuesday, 07 June 2022 09:22 To: Danie Bantjes <danie.bantjes@gmail.com> Cc: JAX@REGIMA.COM; PETE@REGIMA.COM; Jacqui Faucitt <jax@regima.zone> Subject: Exports - SARS   Hi Danie   Pete just want you to have a look at this explanation, and then we will discuss on Thursday.   Regards

All pretty straight forward.   There are only two payments to be made (per attached IRP6 returns) Regima Skin Treatments - R286,222.44 Regima WWD - R2377.48 Please let me know if it will be in order for me to load the payments for 31st August 2022?     Kind regards,   Danie Bantjes     On Mon, 22 Aug 2022 at 13:39, Rynette Farrar < rynette@regima.zone > wrote: Hi Danie   Salaries of R100k per month for the three confirmed – also see attachment.   TB’s attached.   Shout if you need anything else.   Regards     From: Danie Bantjes < danie.bantjes@gmail.com > Sent: Monday, 22 August 2022 10:47 To: Rynette Farrar < rynette@regima.zone >; Jacqui Faucitt < jax@regima.zone >; Peter Andrew Faucitt < pete@regima.com > Subject: Provisional Tax 1st Period 2023   Morning Rynette   It is our "favourite" time of the tax year again. We need to pay provisional tax at the end of August 2022, being the first period in the 2023 Tax year.   I have done my provisional calculations but please just confirm the following for me: Please confirm the Gross Salaries for Pete, Jacqui and Danny as from March 2022 to the end of August (still R100k per month)? Please send me a provisional TB's as at 31 July 2022, for Regima Skin Treatments/RWWD as well as Strategic Logistics. Thanks very much!   Kind regards,   Danie Bantjes

Does this VAT no belong to any of our companies? Kind regards, Danie Bantjes

subject: Villa Via Top Up payment
body_text: Morning everyone  As discussed we need to do a top-up provisional tax payment for Villa Via for the 2022 tax year I've used the following calculation: Net profit per TB                             R4,646,083 Estimated depreciation                   R-180,000 Estimated Taxable income              R4,466,083 Must be within 80%                        R3,572,867 Tax @ 28%                                     R1,000,403 Therefore we must please pay R1,000,403 to SARS by doing the following: See the highlighted part below The reference number to use is very important, which is: 9636866841P00032022 We need to pay this before the end of the month. Please let me know if you have any problems with this? Kind regards, Danie Bantjes

subject: TB's till January 2023
Stock control
body_text: Hi Danie   Ek het geen idea waar hierdie totale vandaan kom nie.  Jy kan sien alles lyk reg tot die 17de September dan begin iets verkeerd gaan. Ook op die 30ste September en dan weer op die 28ste February.  Kyk op die 1ste aanhangsel waar ek gemerk het in rooi.    Ek onthou ek het eenkeer iets gesien wat nie reg was nie, en het dit reggemaak – sien my journal op die 28ste February.   Maar die Pomegranate olie is nog steeds verkeerd – ook in hierdie jaar – sien hieronder       Die ander raws soos die Celldetox, Detoxyl, Regu-stretch lyk almal nou reg in die jaar.  Dit is net die Pomegranante olie wat uit plek lyk die jaar so ver.   As ek kyk na die bill of materials (2de aanhangsel) lyk die raw pryse tog vir my reg op die bills, so ek weet regtig nie waar die vreeslike syfers vandaan kom nie.   Wil jy h ê dat ek die ou van Pastel kry dat hy kan kom kyk wat aangaan hier?   Groete

subject: Fica for RegimA Skin Treatments
body_text: Hi Rynette Sorry to do this to you, but could you please complete the attached form and put together the below documents. This is for the legal advice Pete is getting on a transaction. My Regulatory Team will require the following FICA documentation for Régima Skin Treatments CC, to ensure that we meet the legal and regulatory policies placed on our firm in terms of the Financial Intelligence Centre Act 31 of 2001:   - Signed copy of our Engagement Letter (Attached) - Complete FICA Questionnaire (Attached) - Company CK Forms or any other reliable document - Proof of Operating Address (Company Letterhead or Utility Bill [Not older than 3 months]) - Received - Proof of Tax Number (SARS Document showing the Tax Number [Not older than 3 months]) - Proof of VAT Number (SARS Document showing the VAT Number [Not older than 3 months]) - Proof of Bank Details (Bank Confirmation Letter or Bank Statement [Not older than 3 months]) - ID/Passport/Smart Card of all Members - Proof of Address in respect of all Members (Not older than 3 months) - Resolution confirming authorised person to act on behalf of the CC (I will do this) - ID/Passport/Smart Card & Proof of Address of authorised person Kind regards, Danie Bantjes

subject: SARS - RLA
body_text: Hi Danie   Please see letter from SARS.  They want a letter from us explaining why we didn’t register for the RLA, within 21 days.   They have quite a cheek putting changes on their website, email no letters to us, and then they want to suspend our import license.  Do they think we have nothing better to do, then to stare at their website all day long?   Will you do the letter?   I have tried to go onto Efiling, but I’m not the administrator, so you will have to create the access for me so that I can use the portal going forward.    I guess I will then also register at the same time, so that when we send the letter, they can see we have complied.   I have attached the instructions on how to do this – see page 2.   Regards

subject: Danie SARS is looking for you
body_text: Hi Danie   Please see letter below.  SARS wants to come and have a coffee with you J   Regards       From: Melodie Koen < mkoen@sars.gov.za > Sent: Thursday, November 16, 2023 2:36 PM To: pete@regima.com Cc: Azwifaneli Matshili < AMatshili@sars.gov.za > Subject: FIRST PROVISIONAL TAX PERIOD FOR 2024: VILLA VIA ARCADIA NO 2 CC Importance: High   Dear Sir / Madam   Hope you are well.   The company ‘Villa Via Arcadia No 2 CC’ was selected to review the provisional tax submitted for the first 2024 period.   Please send me the documents and information requested in the attach letter within the next 10 business days (on or before 30 November 2023).   Your assistance will be appreciated.   Kind regards,   Mrs Melodie Koen Focused Segments Email: mkoen@sars.gov.za     Disclaimer Please Note: This email and its contents are subject to our email legal notice which can be viewed at https://www.sars.gov.za/terms-and-conditions/email-disclaimer/

subject: SARS Case No - 473916580
body_text: Good morning Danie   I tried to register on SARS Efiling for this customs registration.  We only have 21 days in which to do it, and quite a few days have passed already.  Remember this was the notice where they threatened to de-register our customs code and I wrote an explanation letter why they should not do so.   I thought I would do all three companies quickly this morning, but I got the same message below on Strategic, WWD and RegimA.         So, can you see if you log in with your details, if you can register them perhaps?   This link - Video link : How to navigate the RLA system via YouTube https://youtu.be/kN-sZWOFkpw - shows you exactly how to do it.   Please let me know if you have succeeded.   Regards       From: RLARegistrations <RLARegistrations@sars.gov.za> Sent: Saturday, November 4, 2023 4:14 PM To: Rynette Farrar <rynette@regima.zone> Subject: RE: Case No - 473916580   Good day   The below email is well received and noted. However, please be notified that you are required to on board your existing Customs Code to RLA System within 21 days from the date you have received the letter of notice of intent to suspend.   The Registration, Licensing and Accreditation (RLA) self-service automated system is open to all clients to migrate to the RLA system. It is a legislative requirement that all Customs and Excise registrants and licensees update their company or individual details captured on the South African Revenue Service’s (SARS) Customs and Excise register. All our existing clients are encouraged to migrate to the new live system.   Kindly find attached the guides on how to on board to RLA System. Please make sure that all client types are on boarded to RLA.   Alternatively, click on the Youtube video link below on how to navigate RLA:   Video link : How to navigate the RLA system via YouTube https://youtu.be/kN-sZWOFkpw   Kind reg

subject: Customs registration
body_text: Hi Danie   Just want to know if you have come right with the customs registration?   Regards           From: Rynette Farrar Sent: Friday, November 24, 2023 6:51 AM To: Danie Bantjes <danie.bantjes@gmail.com> Cc: Jacqui Faucitt <jax@regima.zone>; Peter Faucitt <PETE@REGIMA.COM> Subject: SARS Case No - 473916580   Good morning Danie   I tried to register on SARS Efiling for this customs registration.  We only have 21 days in which to do it, and quite a few days have passed already.  Remember this was the notice where they threatened to de-register our customs code and I wrote an explanation letter why they should not do so.   I thought I would do all three companies quickly this morning, but I got the same message below on Strategic, WWD and RegimA.         So, can you see if you log in with your details, if you can register them perhaps?   This link - Video link : How to navigate the RLA system via YouTube https://youtu.be/kN-sZWOFkpw - shows you exactly how to do it.   Please let me know if you have succeeded.   Regards       From: RLARegistrations < RLARegistrations@sars.gov.za > Sent: Saturday, November 4, 2023 4:14 PM To: Rynette Farrar < rynette@regima.zone > Subject: RE: Case No - 473916580   Good day   The below email is well received and noted. However, please be notified that you are required to on board your existing Customs Code to RLA System within 21 days from the date you have received the letter of notice of intent to suspend.   The Registration, Licensing and Accreditation (RLA) self-service automated system is open to all clients to migrate to the RLA system. It is a legislative requirement that all Customs and Excise registrants and licensees update their company or individual details captured on the South African Revenue Service’s (SARS) Customs and Excise register. All our existing clients

Kind regards, Danie Bantjes On Tue, 24 Jan 2023 at 15:30, Rynette Farrar <rynette@regima.zone> wrote: Jax, Danie need your tax certificate for February 2022.  Below you will find the link to get your tax certificate, but I do not have you log in details.  Can you please download, and forward to me.   Tks!       From: Jacqui Faucitt <jax@regima.zone> Sent: Friday, 14 October 2022 20:32 To: Rynette Farrar <rynette@regima.zone> Subject: FW: Your Discovery Health Medical Scheme tax certificate - Mrs J Faucitt (014613340) -MailRef#2717419138#-       From: taxcertificate@discovery.co.za < taxcertificate@discovery.co.za > Sent: Thursday, 13 October 2022 11:55 AM To: jax@regima.com Subject: Your Discovery Health Medical Scheme tax certificate - Mrs J Faucitt (014613340) -MailRef#2717419138#-   Membership number: 014613340 Dear Mrs Faucitt Your medical scheme tax certificate If you have not yet submitted your tax return this year or alternatively accepted your auto assessment by SARS, you may need a copy of your latest medical scheme tax certificate. You can find your latest medical scheme tax certificate on the Discovery website. Below you will find the South African Revenue Service’s dates for individual income tax return filling: 1 July to 24 October 2022: Taxpayers who file online 1 July to 24 October 2022: Taxpayers who cannot file electronically can do so at a SARS branch by appointment only 1 July 2022 to 23 January 2023: Provisional taxpayers who file electronically . Ask Discovery for your tax certificates and tax summary using WhatsApp . Save our WhatsApp number 0860 756 756 on your phone and just say "Hi" to start chatting with us. Download the Discovery app or visit our website www.discovery.co.za . Regards Mujeeb Bray Head of Service Discovery Health

body_text: Hi Danie   Ek het jou probreer bel, maar geen luck.   Ons het dieselfde gehad die jaar voor die een.  Onthou ons het hierna gekyk, en ek dink ons conclusion was dat die manufacturing was nie reg opgestel op die system nie.  Ons moes nog daarna gekyk het.  Ek weet nie of dit Bernadine was wat dit gedoen het nie, maar sy het ook ‘n klomp zero’s ingevoeg by die decimal komma’s, wat nie daar hoort nie.  Ek dink ons sal moet kyk hoe dit opgestel is, of dit reg is en dan moet ons dalk die figures wat gestoor is, en obviously verkeerd is, override na die regte totaal.  En meen as jy nou enige iets doen met byvoorbeeld die Oxygeskin, dan gaan al die boekhou bene verkeerd wees want die kos prys hier is heeltemal verkeerd.       Kyk hieronder – there is no ways that hierdie raw materials soveel kos nie, maar as jy die raw material kode gebruik is dit wat mens kry.  En meen die supplier invoices word reg toegevoer, anders sou ons hulle miljoene geskuld het, wat nie die geval is nie.  So, ek is nie heeltemal seker wat in die agtergrond aangaan nie, maar dit is obvious dat iets gekeerd is.             Nou as jy kyk by die stock vir die raw materials op die TB – dit lyk ook nie reg nie.  Daar is no ways dat die voorraad vir die raw materials soveel is nie.     Die problem soos ek dit sien het iets te doen met die ‘manufacturing’ wat opgestel is.  Ek weet nie hoekom scrap ons nie die lot nie, want ons manufacture nie op die oomblik nie.  Ek weet regtig nie hoekom dit in die eerste plek geimplement was nie.   Ek dink ons moet die stock soos ontvang inlees en klaar.  Dit is vir al die raws, en die finished goods.  Wanneer ons wel manufacture, dan dink ek ons moet op Danny se system werk en hopelik sal ons nie sulke lelike gogga’s soos hier kry nie.   Laat weet my wat dink jy.   Groete         From: Danie Bantjes <danie.ban

subject: Re: Strategic Logistics
body_text: Hi Rynette Ok, kom ons chat more wanneer ek daar by julle is. subject: FW: SARS Reminder: Company Income Tax returns (ITR14) - Corplo 2304 CC
body_text: Hi Danie   Some more work for you.   Thanks       From: Jacqui Faucitt <jax@regima.zone> Sent: Wednesday, February 21, 2024 8:48 AM To: Rynette Farrar <rynette@regima.zone> Subject: FW: SARS Reminder: Company Income Tax returns (ITR14)       From: South African Revenue Service < noreply@sars.gov.za > Sent: Wednesday, February 21, 2024 8:10 AM To: jacqui.faucitt@regima.com Subject: SARS Reminder: Company Income Tax returns (ITR14)   Dear CORPCLO 2304, 9823660148,     This is a friendly reminder that your Company Income Tax Return (ITR14) for the 2023 year of assessment is due on 29 February 2024 .   SARS records reflect that your company is operating and has monetary transactions and/or assets linked to your business. We are noting with concern that you are not submitting your Company Income Tax return (ITR14) and therefore not honouring your filing obligations in terms of the Income Tax Act.   It is a legal obligation for registered companies to submit their annual ITR14 returns, by the relevant due dates. Companies must submit the ITR14 return to SARS, in the prescribed manner, twelve months after their financial year-end.   In addition to the annual ITR14 return, companies must also submit provisional tax returns (IRP6) to SARS. The first IRP6 return must be submitted, and payment made within six months of the start of the year of assessment. The second IRP6 must be submitted, and payment made by no later than the last working day of the year of assessment.   If your company does not submit all income-tax return(s) by 29 February 2024 , then SARS may generate an estimated assessment and penalty based on the information at our disposal.   More information If you need help to complete your company income-tax return (ITR14): Visit our YouTube channel, SARS TV (@sarstax), and watch the video, "How to Submit Your Income Tax Returns for Companies (ITR14) on eFiling&quo

subject: Logged in as you
body_text: Hi Danie   Just to let you know I couldn’t come right with SARS logged in as ‘me’ to change the banking details to ABSA.   So, I logged in as you and all is sorted now.   Just so you know in case you get notifications from SARS, that it was me J   I also updated the old post box numbers, to the street address @ Van Buuren.   Thanks

subject: Re: ACCOUNTING OFFICERS REPORT
body_text: Hi Jacqui & Pete All good my side, thanks. Congratulations on the new land purchase. Certainly a prime property! I am equally confused about the certificate the conveyancers want. It actually makes no sense at all. They speak about " the facts in the above certificate". Does this refer to paragraphs 1 and 2? Which then means I simply have to verify that I am the Accounting Officer to Villa Via and that Villa Via's registration number is correct. I have given it my best shot - see attached letter. Please check if this is what they are looking for? Kind regards, Danie Bantjes On Thu, 25 Apr 2024 at 16:27, Jacqui Faucitt <jax@regima.zone> wrote:   Hi Danie   We have just purchased the property next door to 50 Van Buuren through VILLA VIA ARCADIA NO. 2.  Could you please complete the “Accounting Officers Report” attached for the conveying attorneys.  I don’t understand what they are asking for, but I am sure you will have more idea!   How is everything your side?  We must speak soon regarding Rezonance progress.   Kind regards   Pete

subject: Various SARS matters
body_text: Hi Rynette I have to submit the Villa Via tax return today (30th). Please let Pete sign the front page of the attached Annual Financial Statements and then just return that one page back to me. Then, while I was on e-Filing I saw SARS is waiting for a bank verification to be uploaded. Not sure if you're aware of it, here's the letter attached as well. Happy for you to send this to me then I will upload the confirmation. Please also tell Pete happy 72nd birthday for me and please ask Jax to give Pete an extra long hug and a kiss from me! 😁 Kind regards, Danie Bantjes

subject: Fwd: Request for completion of Business Register Quality Improvement Survey 2024 questionnaire
body_text: Hi Rynette I received this from STATS SA relating to RWWD. Kind regards, Danie Bantjes ---------- Forwarded message --------- From: Christina Matshidi < ChristinaMal@statssa.gov.za > Date: Tue, 30 Apr 2024 at 11:44 Subject: Request for completion of Business Register Quality Improvement Survey 2024 questionnaire To: < DANIE.BANTJES@gmail.com > Request for completion of Business Register Quality Improvement Survey 2024 questionnaire     Dear Respondent,   You are hereby requested to provide    information contained in the attached form. The requested information will be used by Statistics South Africa (Stats SA) to update the details of your business in the Statistical Business Register (SBR) database. The updated SBR is the pillar of economic statistics and is used to sample businesses to participate in economic surveys conducted by Stats SA. The official statistics produced by Stats SA help organs of state, businesses, organisations and the public in planning, monitoring, evaluation and decision-making.   The information provided will be kept with high confidentiality and it is used for statistical purpose only. This is according to the Statistics Act of 1999. section 17: Confidentiality and disclosure.   The full act is obtainable on our website, www.statssa.gov.za .   Note that according to the Statistics Act it is compulsory for all business to provide requested information to Statistics South Africa. Kindly follow this link: https://migration.statssa.gov.za/Fieldworkerverification/home.php ,   for Stats SA Employee verification. My Verification number is 09837 .   Please complete the attached form using your latest financial year figures and return it to Stats SA not later than 17 May 2024   Thank you in advance for your co-operation.     Regards   Christina Matshidi| Business Register Officer Business Register (Wing 2, second floor) Statistics South Africa Isibalo House, 75 Koch

subject: Re: SARS correspondence - RegimA Skin Company tax
body_text: Hi Rynette Thanks. This is very odd and highly suspicious. 1. The letter is addressed to you, yet uses the Income Tax Reference number of Regima Skin Treatments. This is highly irregular as SARS never does that, the letter would be addressed to the actual taxpayer. 2. It uses the Income Tax reference number of the company, yet mentions "employer" in the letter in which case the PAYE reference number should have been quoted. 2. This letter should be on e-filing but isn't. Please check if there's anything on the PAYE profile? 3. As we can see from the attached Statement of Account, there is absolutely no "unallocated payments" reflecting anywhere for Income Tax. Maybe just as a last check, please would you pull a PAYE statement of account from e-filing, to check if there are any unallocated payments maybe reflecting on there? Kind regards, Danie Bantjes On Mon, 10 Jun 2024 at 14:00, Rynette Farrar <rynette@regima.zone> wrote:   From: Danie Bantjes < danie.bantjes@gmail.com > Sent: Monday, June 10, 2024 1:53 PM To: Rynette Farrar <rynette@regima.zone> Cc: Jacqui Faucitt <jax@regima.zone> Subject: Re: SARS correspondence - RegimA Skin Company tax   Hi Rynette   Cannot open document?   Kind regards,   Danie Bantjes     On Mon, 10 Jun 2024 at 09:41, Rynette Farrar < rynette@regima.zone > wrote: Hi Danie   Sien asseblief die korrespondensie van SARS vir RegimA Skin's company tax.   Wonder hoekom het hulle my naam nou op vir die company tax?   Groete           -----Original Message----- From: noreply@sars.gov.za < noreply@sars.gov.za > Sent: Monday, June 10, 2024 9:33 AM To: Rynette Farrar < rynette@regima.zone > Subject: 7420716336   Kind Regards   The information transmitted is intended only for the person or entity to which it is addressed and may contain confidential and/or privileged material. This is highly irregular as SARS never does that, the letter would be addressed to the actual taxpayer. 2. It uses the Income Tax reference number of the company, yet mentions "employer" in the letter in which case the PAYE reference number should have been quoted. 2. This letter should be on e-filing but isn't. Please check if there's anything on the PAYE profile? 3. As we can see from the attached Statement of Account, there is absolutely no "unallocated payments" reflecting anywhere for Income Tax.   Maybe just as a last check, please would you pull a PAYE statement of account from e-filing, to check if there are any unallocated payments maybe reflecting on there?       Kind regards,   Danie Bantjes     On Mon, 10 Jun 2024 at 14:00, Rynette Farrar < rynette@regima.zone > wrote:   From: Danie Bantjes < danie.bantjes@gmail.com > Sent: Monday, June 10, 2024 1:53 PM To: Rynette Farrar < rynette@regima.zone > Cc: Jacqui Faucitt < jax@regima.zone > Subject: Re: SARS correspondence - RegimA Skin Company tax   Hi Rynette   Cannot open document?   Kind rega

subject: Beneficial Ownership of Trusts
body_text: Good day everyone Please see below regarding beneficial ownership of trusts. I trust this is taken care of by your advisers on The Peter Faucitt Family Trust? On the 20 th of October 2024 the Department of Justice and Constitutional Development hosted a webinar wherein trustees were reminded on their responsibility to submit the Trust Beneficial Ownership declarations with the Master of the High Court in which the trust is registered in order to ensure compliance with the provisions of the amended Trust Property Control Act.    The deadline for the filing of the beneficial ownership register with the Master of the High Court is the 15 November 2024 , with a grace period up until 30 November 2024   Trustees who are found guilty of non-compliance will face harsh punishment such as; The Trustees may not be eligible to be appointed as a Trustees in respect of other Trust/s. The Trustees may be subject to a fine of up to R10 million. The Trustees may be subject to imprisonment for up to a period of 5 years. The Trustees could be subject to both a fine and imprisonment. We urge you not to take this matter lightly.   See link below for the media statement by the Department of Justice and Constitutional Development: https://www.justice.gov.za/m_statements/2024/20241028-Advisory-Trust-Beneficial-Ownership.html Kind regards, Danie Bantjes

Kind regards, Danie Bantjes On Mon, 9 Dec 2024 at 12:37, Rynette Farrar <rynette@regima.zone> wrote: Good afternoon Danie   Can you please forward me a signed copy of the financials for 2023?  I do not have one on file.   Regards           From: Danie Bantjes < danie.bantjes@gmail.com > Sent: Monday, December 9, 2024 11:12 AM To: Rynette Farrar <rynette@regima.zone> Cc: Jacqui Faucitt <jax@regima.zone>; Peter Faucitt < PETE@regima.com > Subject: Re: FW: 9636866841 VILLA VIA ARCADIA NO 2 CC   Hi Rynette   Please see my response that should be sent to them.   However, before you send it, please ask them why the letter is not appearing on e-Filing, which allows for a response and documents to be uploaded.   As there is no such correspondence on e-filing, I'm not sure we should send anything without having a record of this on e-Filing.       Kind regards,   Danie Bantjes     On Thu, 5 Dec 2024 at 15:44, Rynette Farrar < rynette@regima.zone > wrote: Hi Danie   Please see the letter from SARS re Prov Tax - 202501.   Thanks       From: Lungisani Khena < LKhena@sars.gov.za > Sent: Thursday, December 5, 2024 3:37 PM To: pete@regima.com ; Rynette Farrar < rynette@regima.zone > Cc: Azwifaneli Matshili < AMatshili@sars.gov.za > Subject: 9636866841 VILLA VIA ARCADIA NO 2 CC   Dear Sir/Madam,   Kindly find the attached letter for your attention.   Please ensure that all documentation requested in the letter is included in order to assess and finalise the process.   Kindly submit the information on or before 19 December 2024 if the Company does not form part of Group of Companies that is listed and published results are awaited.   Your co-operation in this regard will be highly appreciated.     Regards   Lungisani Andile Khe

subject: Re: Personal Income Tax - 2024
body_text: Hi everyone Apologies for all the scurrying for tax documentation, but all the personal tax returns for 2024 have now been lodged well in time. Attached the assessments issued. Jacqui is to expect a refund of R53k. The assessment may be subject to a verification process but if all goes well, the refund should be made shortly. Please just confirm that you still have the FNB account as your personal account? Danny needs to pay in R18k, due to his investment income. We have until 28 February 2025 to pay, I will load the payment closer to that date and let alert you that you should have sufficient funds in  your account at FNB. Pete needs to pay in R41k, due to investment income as well as the Momentum Annuity.  We have until 28 February 2025 to pay, I will load the payment closer to that date and let alert you that you should have sufficient funds in  your account at FNB. subject: SARS verification queries
to: Rynette Farrar <rynette@regima.zone>, Peter Andrew Faucitt <pete@regima.com>, Jacqui Faucitt <jax@regima.zone>, Linda <linda@regima.zone>
body_text: Hi Rynette Here's the SARS verification letters. We need to respond by 31st March 2025. The questions are a bit tricky, especially the Strategic Logistics one, so we need to be careful not to

subject: SARS verification queries
body_text: Hi Rynette Here's the SARS verification letters. We need to respond by 31st March 2025. The questions are a bit tricky, especially the Strategic Logistics one, so we need to be careful not to elicit further, deeper investigations by SARS. The question revolves around why the company is making a "Gross Loss", i.e. why are the direct costs exceeding the Revenue in this company. During our recent discussions on the financial statements, I did raise this. You and Pete's response was that Strategic Logistics is "just a conduit". Please think about a feasible answer that gives this statement more substance, and then why would the direct costs still exceed Revenue?  I will field the question on RWWD as to why we overpaid provisional tax in that year. Kind regards, Danie Bantjes

subject: SARS verification queries - Strategic - Case Number 529115858
body_text: Good afternoon Danie   Pete and I had a discussion regarding this loss in Strategic.   I recall that there was a meeting between yourself and Pete, and somehow, I also think Danny was present, where a discussion took place, and after this meeting I was told to no longer charge the admin fees between the sister companies.   Nobody told me the reason for it, but ever since that day, I have complied with this instruction.   If I look at the financial statements and compare it to the previous year being 2023, then one can clearly see that R1 100 000.00 was received as Admin Fee in 2023, whereas in 2024 no admin fees were received.   Therefore, if Strategic was receiving the admin fees as before, there would not have been a loss.   Pete don’t recall the reason for dropping the admin fees either, and he asked me to run this by you.   If I look at the comparative figures of the sales vs the COS, then those figures make sense, and you don’t need to be a rocket scientist to understand this.  The sales increased by R1 673 436 and the COS increased by R1 8322 259.  The difference obviously that of rising costs of materials.   So, I think we should concentrate as a reason for the loss of profit, on the fact that Strategic no longer charges the admin fees.  I don’t know if you want to use maybe as a reason for this, the fact that the Regima Skin Treatment’s loan account with Strategic dropped from R19 997 205 in 2023, to R0.00 in 2024. I don’t know if one can say that Regima Skin was given a break in the admin fees in order for them to get their loan account down to acceptable levels.   Pete asks that you please answer this one, as you can better articulate this argument to SARS.   Regards                   From: Danie Bantjes <danie.bantjes@gmail.com> Sent: Sunday, March 9, 2025 12:13 PM To: Rynette Farrar <rynette@regima.zone>; Peter Andrew Faucitt <pete

body_text: Hi Rynette Thanks for the reminder! Here's the reply I submitted. Short and to the point. Crossing fingers that SARS will be satisfied with this. I steered away from using the lack of admin fees,

subject: Re: SARS verification queries - Strategic - Case Number 529115858
body_text: Hi Rynette Thanks for the reminder! Here's the reply I submitted. Short and to the point. Crossing fingers that SARS will be satisfied with this. I steered away from using the lack of admin fees, as this is a dangerous subject with SARS frowning on this as a method of juggling profits within a group of related companies. Going forward we should have a closer look at this and I suggest we charge a quarterly Cost (not an admin fee, perhaps a printing, packaging and/or marketing fee) across from RST equal to circa 2.5% of the revenue for that quarter, so that SL makes a small profit. subject: TB's after stock adjustments - February 2025 - Strategic / Regima Skin / WWD
body_text: Good afternoon Danie   Herewith the TB’s after I have done all the stock adjustments.   You must have a look at Strategic.  I stopped with that system that Bernadine introduce by manufacturing the stock received from Proficos and Prime.   Obviously, the stock doesn’t adjust then during the year, but have to be adjusted after proper stock taking at the end of the year.   The stock as I have it now on the stock valuation, is the figures as per a proper stock count.    If I look on the Pastel system end of February 2024, then on the stock valuation it is zero for finished goods, and which is correct.  But if I look at the TB it shows finished goods as R1 443 217.78   It I go and look where this comes from, I can see it have something to do with Bernadine’s manufacturing bills.  So, not to sure what’s going on here.   The finished goods at the end of February 2024 are in credit by R2 756 321.85.  There are no negative figures if one look at the stock valuation.  The finished goods on the stock valuation are at zero.   I did discuss with Pete briefly, and he said that we will have to sit down and strategize.   I think you and me must also have a look at the Pastel system and see how we can permanently remove this Bernadine gogga J for good!   Have a wonderful wet weekend.   Regards

subject: Villa Via Arcadia No2 CC
body_text: Hi Rynette I have to submit the 2024 tax return together with these financial statements today, being the deadline. With all the public holidays this month, I've run out of time to come visit you, as usual, to get Pete's signature on the financials.  In short, the financial results for 2024 are pretty much the same as the previous year, so I see no potential issues from a SARS perspective. In terms of tax, I see one-filing that we never made a second provisional tax payment for the 2024 tax year. Therefore we will owe SARS circa R630k when the final assessment is raised for the 2024 tax year. This will be payable by the end of March 2025. Will let you know as soon as that is received. Please get Pete to sign the first page and scan the entire pack back to me. I'll come visit shortly to provide you with the final adjusting journal entries we need to pass, which we must do before you roll over to the new financial year. Kind regards, Danie Bantjes

Kind regards, Danie Bantjes ---------- Forwarded message --------- From: Danie Bantjes < danie.bantjes@gmail.com > Date: Mon, 26 May 2025 at 15:05 Subject: Regima Group of Companies To: Jacqui Faucitt <jax@regima.zone>, Daniel Faucitt < d@rzo.io >, Peter Andrew Faucitt < pete@regima.com > Dear Jacqui, Danny and Peter Regarding the uncertainties surrounding the computer expenses as well as the financial status of the Regima Group of Companies, I would like to suggest the following: 1.       I prefer not to meet with individual parties regarding this matter to maintain my impartiality. Instead, I will present a factual report on: o    Consolidated External Revenues for the past two years o    Consolidated Net Trading Profits or Losses for the past two financial years for the entire group o    Computer expenses over the past two financial years o    The consolidated bank balances across the Group. 2. Computer Expenses – details: Attached is a schedule of all computer expenses allocated by Rynette for March and April 2025. Danny to kindly provide the following next to each expense: Description of the expense (licenses, usage costs, etc.) in layman’s terms. Current or future business application. Justification from SARS’ perspective as “ in the production of income.” This serves a dual purpose, as I'm expecting SARS to audit our computer expenses claimed for tax, so this gets us ready for such an audit. I trust this will be in order. Kind regards, Danie Bantjes

subject: RegimA World Wide position
body_text: Good morning Danie   Following our recent telecon re RWWD, as I understand the situation, come the time that we prepare the 2024/2025 financials for this company, we are on course to post a substantial loss, as we did in 2023/2024, and likely in 2025/2026 should the current situation continue.   At this point SARS will probably order an in-depth audit.  During the process of this audit they will almost certainly ask the following questions:   Are we in possession of physical bona fide invoices for the expenditure?   Was the expenditure incurred necessary for the efficient operation of the company i.e. did it increase sales, reduce manpower requirements / simplify the overall operation of the company, or result in any new market innovations which would result in increased revenue in the future?   If the answer to the above questions is negative, SARS will then deem a substantial amount of this loss as “personal expenditure” which will force the directors of the company to inject personal assets into the company to cover the assessed losses, and may also result in SARS casting a wider net into the other companies in the group with possible similar outcomes.  The above summary raises several questions:   Is my assessment of the risk a realistic scenario?   Are there any other outcomes which may materialize from SARS or other entities that I haven’t covered?   What is your considered professional advice on the above matter?   I understand that the recent “questionnaire” you requested us to answer which should be completed by Monday, will give more insight into the depth of the hole we now find ourselves in.   Regards     pp Pete

subject: Re: Villa Via Arcadia No 2 CC - Ref No: 9636866841
body_text: Dear Hubert That is correct, we used the 2024 Income Statement as the basis for the estimation of taxable income for the 202502 provisional tax payment. That was the best estimate we had at the time of submitting the second provisional tax return for the 2025 tax year. The 2025 financial year has just recently closed and we have not yet prepared any financial statements for this financial year, as we have 12 months from 30 April 2025 to prepare and file these financial statements. I trust you find this in order. Kind regards, Danie Bantjes On Thu, 3 Jul 2025 at 17:22, Hubert Nkosi < hnkosi@sars.gov.za > wrote: Good day Danie   Thank for the response to the first letter.   Kindly submit the AFS (Income Statement and Balance Sheet) for period ending 30 April 2025.   The provided AFS are for the year ended 30 April 2024.   Kind regards,   Hubert Nkosi Taxpayer Engagement Gauteng: Auditor: Tax Verifications CIT _____________________________________________________ Tel: +27 (0) 12 018 0063 e-mail: hnkosi@sars.gov.za 4 Rissik Street | Marshalltown | Johannesburg | 2001   www.sars.gov.za     From: Danie Bantjes < danie.bantjes@gmail.com > Sent: 30 June 2025 13:38 To: Hubert Nkosi < hnkosi@sars.gov.za >; Rynette Farrar <rynette@regima.zone> Subject: Villa Via Arcadia No 2 CC - Ref No: 9636866841   CAUTION: This email originated from outside the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.   Dear Hubert Nkosi   Your letter dated 10 June 2025 refers.   Please see attached letter in response to your queries and the 2024 Financial Statements for the taxpayer, for your kind attention.   We trust this will be in order.     Kind regards,   Danie Bantjes
